behavior_summary: Responds to player 'U8P_GUARD7::look' (examine) by checking whether the guard NPC is dead and barking either a live label ('guardsman') or a dead label ('dead guardsman').

When the player enters the guard's fast-area (enterFastArea), the script will revive and reposition a dead guard (clear dead/in-combat flags and teleport to a fixed tile) or, if alive, spawn short behavior scripts to run immediate reactions.

Provides an interactive 'use' handler that opens a small question menu, chooses dialog branches based on story/global flags (guard7Met, guard7Pissed, guard7Thanked, devonInRule) and on a local 'freebies' counter (assembled from hydrosFree, hasHeart, pyrosFree), uses urandom to vary some replies, and spawns follow-up behavior.

Contains a scheduled task (schedule) that spawns a timing method; depending on the spawn result it may clear the dead flag and spawn another follow-up method (suggests periodic revive-check or patrol behavior).
Interacts heavily with engine services (Npc::isDead, Npc::clrDead, Npc::teleport, Item::bark, Item::ask, spawn) to implement guard labeling, revival, dialog, and deferred behaviors.
notable_control_flow: Multiple Npc::isDead checks gate core behavior: U8P_GUARD7::look uses it to select label string; enterFastArea and schedule use it to decide revive vs. spawn behaviors.
GUARD7::use builds a local counter (BP-06h) from global flags hydrosFree, hasHeart, pyrosFree; counter>0 forces an 'alarm/urgent' branch instead of the normal dialog tree.

Dialog branches in use are driven by guard7Met and guard7Pissed: the first meeting sets guard7Met; an angry branch clears guard7Pissed after barking; some branches set guard7Thanked to prevent repeating a thank-you message.
Several behaviors are deferred using spawn; schedule inspects spawn return/result to determine whether to revive (clrDead) and to spawn a second method.
Item::ask returns a string (stored in BP-02h) that is compared against known questions to select the proper reply; the slist is created/freed around the ask call.
open_questions: Exact boolean/return convention of Npc::isDead in this engine excerpt: branches imply the interpretation but conventions vary across scripts (appears to be reliable but noted as an inference).
Precise behavior and content of spawned methods (METHOD::091C, METHOD::15E7, METHOD::143A, METHOD::133F, METHOD::1090, METHOD::0BCF) — the script defers many operations to these unknown routines.
Exact teleport coordinates and flags passed to Npc::teleport when reviving in enterFastArea (the disassembly shows constants but not their in-game meaning).
What exact strings are presented by all ask/options variants and the full set of randomized replies produced by urandom-driven branches (we only have label identifiers and partial string text).
Whether guard revival on enterFastArea is intended as a persistent auto-respawn or a situational reposition (behavior depends on how other systems treat clrDead/clrInCombat).

main_function.name: U8P_GUARD7::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the 'U8P_GUARD7::look' (examine) event for this guard NPC: determine if the NPC is dead and present an appropriate short bark/label to the player.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_GUARD7::look) — triggered when the Avatar/player looks at or inspects the guard (the engine delivers the target 'process pointer' and viewer pid).

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD7::look
function_map.role: Respond to 'U8P_GUARD7::look' by checking Npc::isDead and calling Item::bark with either the live or dead label.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD7::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Triggered on area entry; if NPC is dead it revives and teleports the NPC; otherwise spawns immediate reaction behaviors.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD7::schedule
function_map.role: Periodic scheduled routine: spawns a timing method and, depending on the spawn result, may clear the dead flag and spawn another follow-up behavior.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD7::use
function_map.role: Handle player 'use' interaction: immediate bark, spawn helpers, compute freebies counter from globals, present an Item::ask menu, branch dialog based on guard7Met/guard7Pissed/guard7Thanked/devonInRule and random choices, and spawn a follow-up method.

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: Query whether the NPC is in the dead state.

function_map.name: Npc::clrInCombat
function_map.role: Clear 'in combat' flag on NPC (used after revive).

function_map.name: Npc::teleport
function_map.role: Teleport/reposition NPC to given coordinates/region and arguments (used after revive in enterFastArea).

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Make the NPC emit a short text line (label/bark) to the player process/pid.

function_map.name: Item::ask
function_map.role: Present a small list of dialog choices to the player and return the chosen string.

function_map.name: spawn
function_map.role: Queue/launch other methods/scripts (behavior, animation, or deferred actions) used throughout the guard script.

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Return a pseudo-random number used to vary some dialog lines in the angry branch.

function_map.name: FREE::1C19
function_map.role: Library/free helper invoked during use(); likely a resource/UI helper (purpose inferred).

function_map.name: FREE::2A56
function_map.role: Library/free helper invoked during use(); likely a resource/UI helper (purpose inferred).

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn a short reaction/behavior when the guard is alive and player enters area.
Spawn an additional short reaction/behavior when the guard is alive and player enters area.
Start a timing/periodic behavior and examine its return to drive further actions.
Spawn follow-up behavior when the earlier spawn returns a particular result (often after clearing dead state).
Spawn an immediate helper action when the guard is used (UI or state helper before dialog).
Queue a follow-up behavior after certain dialog branches complete.

external_calls.purpose: Check whether to use the 'dead guardsman' label or the live 'guardsman' label.
Emit the short label/description string to the player/pid.
Decide between reviving+teleporting a dead guard or spawning live reactions.
Revive the NPC if it was dead upon area entry.
Clear combat state after revival to ensure NPC is passive/idle.
Reposition the revived NPC to a fixed tile/region so it resumes intended location.
Possibly revive the NPC when a scheduled spawn indicates it should be cleared of dead flag.
Emit dialog lines such as the immediate 'Guardsman!' bark and follow-up replies.
Present player a choice list (Who are you?/What is your duty?/Where am I?/Bye) and return the selected string for branching.
Select between multiple randomized dialog replies in the angry/alternate greeting branch.
Early helper call during use(); likely UI/resource related (inferred).
Early helper call during use(); likely UI/resource related (inferred).

reads.name: hydrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Contributes to the local freebies counter used by GUARD7::use to decide an alarm/urgent branch.

reads.name: hasHeart
reads.likely_effect: Affects dialog branching in GUARD7::use and contributes to freebies counter; also selects alternate bark text in at least one path.

reads.name: pyrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Contributes to the local freebies counter used by GUARD7::use to decide an alarm/urgent branch.

reads.name: devonInRule
reads.likely_effect: Read in GUARD7::use to decide whether to play a special thank-you dialog about Devon.

reads.name: guard7Met
reads.likely_effect: Determines whether to use the 'first meeting' greeting or a repeat greeting in GUARD7::use.

reads.name: guard7Pissed
reads.likely_effect: Selects an alternate 'angry' greeting branch in GUARD7::use that is later cleared by some branches.

reads.name: guard7Thanked
reads.likely_effect: Indicates whether the guard has already delivered a thank-you reply (used to avoid repeating the thank-you).

writes.name: guard7Pissed
writes.likely_effect: Cleared/set to 0 by at least one dialog path after barking an angry line; resets angry state so normal dialog can resume.

writes.name: guard7Met
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 on the first meeting in GUARD7::use to mark that the player has met this guard.

writes.name: guard7Thanked
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the guard delivers a thank-you response for a story event (devonInRule), preventing repetition.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Responds to player 'U8P_GUARD7::look' (examine) by checking whether the guard NPC is dead and barking either a live label ('guardsman') or a dead label ('dead guardsman').
